turns.thought: [OBSERVE] At iteration 419, the swarm is completely stalled: no improvement for 200 iterations, gbest stuck at 19.8998. Diversity is effectively zero (normalized 1.84e-16), all particles have identical fitness (std 6.86e-15) and zero velocity (velocity_zero_ratio = 1.0). The swarm has collapsed to a single point in a local minimum of the Rastrigin landscape. | [DIAGNOSE] This is severe premature convergence. The root cause is that the swarm lost all diversity and momentum, likely trapped in a deep local basin of the multimodal Rastrigin function. Standard PSO dynamics can no longer produce movement without external perturbation. | [STRATEGIZE] We must reintroduce significant diversity to escape the current basin. Resetting a large fraction of particles randomly across the search space is the most reliable way to break out of total collapse, as any local perturbation (mutation) would likely remain trapped. Using reset_worst_particles with a 0.5 ratio and fresh velocities will disperse half the swarm, allowing the algorithm to discover new basins while preserving the current best as a fallback.
